description.en: Learn for loops by following a postman who delivers letters to 5 houses in order, visiting each one without skipping.
description.ta: 5 वीडुकळिल् कडितं वितरिक्कुम् तपाल्काररै पिन्पिल्लित्तु for loops आल्लतु कल्लुङ्कळ्.

story_variants.en: Meet Rajan, the PyMasters postman. Every morning he walks down a street with 5 houses. He does not skip any house, and he does not revisit a house he has already been to. He simply goes 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 — one house at a time, in order. A Python `for` loop does exactly the same thing: it visits every item in a sequence, once, in order. `range(5)` creates that list of five houses. The loop variable `i` is like Rajan's current door number. When all doors are done, the loop ends.
story_variants.ta: PyMasters तपाल्काररान राजनै संतियुङ्कळ्. कालैयिल् 5 वीडुकळ् उळ्ळ तेरुविल् नडकिन्ऱार्. ओरु वीड्डैयुं विडुवित्ताल्, लिस्ट् तिरुम्पविल्लै. 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 ऐन्नु वरिसैयाक सेल्लुवार्. Python `for` सुषियुम् अव्वाऱे तान् — ओव्वोरु उरुप्पैयुम् वरिसैयाक सेन्तु सिड्डुकिन्ऱतु.
story_variants.te: PyMasters తపాలా వాడు రాజన్ను కలవండి. అతను ప్రతిరోజూ 5 ఇళ్ళములు ఉన్న వీధిలో నడుస్తాడు. ఏ ఇంటిని దాటడు, ఎండుకు వెళ్ళడు. Python `for` loop అదే విధంగా పనిచేస్తుంది.
story_variants.fr: Rencontrez Rajan, le facteur PyMasters. Chaque matin il parcourt une rue avec 5 maisons. Il ne saute aucune maison et n'en revisite aucune. Il fait simplement 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 — une maison à la fois, dans l'ordre. Une boucle `for` Python fait exactement la même chose: elle visite chaque élément d'une séquence, une fois, dans l'ordre. Quand toutes les portes sont faites, la boucle se termine.
story_variants.es: Conoce a Rajan, el cartero de PyMasters. Cada mañana camina por una calle con 5 casas. No se salta ninguna casa y no vuelve a visitar una ya visitada. Simplemente va 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 — una casa a la vez, en orden. Un bucle `for` de Python hace exactamente lo mismo: visita cada elemento de una secuencia, una vez, en orden.
story_variants.ko: PyMasters 우체부간 라잔을 만나보세요. 그는 매일 아침 5개의 집이 있는 거리를 걸어다닙니다. 어떤 집도 건너맰다 너짜지지 않고, 이미 방문한 집은 다시 가지 않습니다. 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 순서대로 한 집씩. Python `for` 루프도 정확히 같습니다.

explanation.en: Start a loop that runs 5 times (i = 0 to 4)
Print the current house number on each iteration
explanation.ta: ओव्वोरु सुषियिलुम् तल्लि वीड्डु ऎण्णै अच्सिडु

animation_sequence.output:
Delivering to house 0
Delivering to house 1
Delivering to house 2
Delivering to house 3
Delivering to house 4

instruction.en: Use a for loop to print the numbers 1 to 5, each on its own line.
instruction.ta: for सुषि उपयोकित्तु 1 लिरुन्तु 5 वरै उळ्ळ ऎण्णुकळै तनित्तनि वरिसैयाक अच्सिडु.

en: range(5) gives 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 — but you need 1 to 5
Try range(1, 6) to start at 1 and stop before 6
The loop variable i holds the current number each iteration
print(i) will print whatever i currently holds
ta: range(5) 0 लिरुन्तु 4 वरै तरुकिन्ऱतु — आनाल् 1 लिरुन्तु 5 वेण्डुम्
सुषि माल्मु i तल्लि तल्लि तानाक हान्त्तिल् कोल्लुकिन्ऱतु

q.en: How many times does the body of `for i in range(4):` execute?
q.ta: `for i in range(4):` इन् तोकुडि ऎत्तनै तडवै इयङ्कुम्?

content.en: Think of iteration like turning pages in a book. Each page is one step. You start at page 1 and keep going until you reach the last page. You never jump backwards. That is exactly what a loop does with range().
content.ta: सुषि ऐन्पतु ओरु नूलिन् ताळ्कळै तिरुप्पुवतु पोल. ओव्वोरु ताळुम् ओरु कडि. कडैसि ताळै वरैयीलुम् तोडङ्कि सेल्लुवार्. वषिडेम् तिरुम्पवुम् वरविल्लै.
